note: all rows consumed by the causal-spine session — 53 seeds typed+verified+minted (or honestly flagged), 38 stubs wired to 0 remaining. Record: working/fire-and-blood/causal-spine-s204/ + apply/fab-causal-spine-s204/.

snippet: Daeron conquered Dorne, but was unable to hold it
note: Daeron I conquers Dorne → cannot hold it → dies young

snippet: Moon's murder removed the last major obstacle
note: Moon's assassination → host disintegrates → way to Oldtown clear → Jaehaerys crowned

snippet: the Widow-lover had lopped off each man’s sword hand
note: maiming of ransomed captives → Aegon's Balerion reprisal on the Wyls

snippet: a yard-long iron bolt caught the queen's dragon in the right eye
note: scorpion kills Meraxes → Rhaenys's death → the Dragon's Wroth

snippet: Helaena had been murdered, the whispers went
note: Helaena's death rumor -> bloody riot of King's Landing

snippet: Seasmoke’s descent upon Riverrun had at last persuaded
note: dragon in courtyard → Elmo Tully calls Tully banners for the queen

snippet: an accord was reached between Lord Borros, Lord Larys, and Queen Alicent
note: tent parley -> restoration of Aegon II to King's Landing

snippet: The Citadel names the clash that followed the Battle of the Kingsroad
note: the Muddy Mess, last battle of the Dance

snippet: You fed his mother to your dragon
note: Aegon III's captivity and the reason he will not spare his uncle

snippet: We shall rule this realm together, you and I
note: Aenys bequeaths Blackfyre to Maegor → later strife between the brothers

snippet: The Iron Throne will go to the man who has the strength to seize it
note: Maegor's usurpation doctrine leads to Gawen's execution and war for the throne

snippet: the young dragon plunged to earth, and Prince Aegon with her
note: Quicksilver downed -> Aegon dies -> rebel host routs

snippet: secret marriage to his sister → deeply at odds with the King’s Hand
note: the secret marriage causes the estrangement from Alyssa and Rogar

snippet: He wanted an alliance with the Iron Throne
note: Lyman's ambition for dragons -> Rhaena resolves to leave the Rock

snippet: a fire in her Jaehaerys does not have
note: Rogar names Aerea claimant -> council turns on him as treason

snippet: That is how we shall win this
note: claim wild-dragon riders leads to nine or twelve dragons vs Aegon's four

snippet: the tidings from King's Landing had driven the princess into a black fury
note: news of Viserys's death and Aegon's crowning leads to Rhaenyra's early labor and stillbirth

snippet: Here. Harrenhal
note: need of a mainland toehold for river friends leads to Daemon's assault on Harrenhal

snippet: An eye for an eye, a son for a son
note: Luke's death → Daemon's raven → Blood and Cheese murder

snippet: Was it one of your eyes he took, or one of your balls?
note: Maris's taunt → Aemond's pursuit of Lucerys

snippet: What happened next remains a matter of some dispute
note: Regis Groves's refusal to kneel -> his death by contested means at Harrenhal

snippet: watched his uncle feed his mother to a dragon
note: Aegon III's trauma -> his rage at any mention of dragons

snippet: crops died in the field
note: cruel winter → crops die → famine → bread prices rise

snippet: against the Shivers | was helpless
note: plague helplessness leads to war as the king's healing

snippet: he could well have the makings of an archmaester in him
note: Elysar's counsel to Vaegon sent to the Citadel

snippet: the Blackwoods kept the old gods
note: Daella learns of weirwood vows to betrothal collapses

snippet: I would never marry her
note: Vaegon's public insult to search for a new bride for Vaegon

snippet: We ought never have pushed her into marriage
note: forced marriage → Daella's death → Alysanne blames Jaehaerys

snippet: missed Lord Cameron...and struck Prince Aemon
note: errant Myrish bolt aimed at the Evenstar kills Aemon

snippet: The Hand names the regent and the regent names the Hand
note: Viserys mocks the circular authority of the regency coup

snippet: a dragon in the skies above the city
note: Rhaena flying Morning renewed hope of the Lysene Spring

snippet: the poison had been baked into the crust of the apple tarts
note: poisoning of Gaemon leads to Aegon III's renewed gloom

snippet: those who tortured him to bring forth this false confession
note: torture of Rowan then false confession then king's counterstroke

snippet: plunged it into Waters's belly
note: Flowers's treachery leads to Marston Waters's death

snippet: You are sitting in my chair
note: Aegon's sixteenth nameday ends the regency

snippet: made an enemy of a man who had been amongst his most leal
note: curt dismissal turns Manderly into an enemy

snippet: My new Hand is a steel fist
note: Otto dismissed then Criston Cole made Hand then executions begin

snippet: Criston Cole had sprung his trap
note: Rook's Rest trap then Meleys and Rhaenys killed, Sunfyre and Aegon maimed

snippet: four new dragonriders had been found
note: the Red Sowing then new riders then assault on King's Landing prepared

snippet: battle was joined
note: Triarchy fleet enters the Gullet -> Battle in the Gullet

snippet: for the rank and file of the City Watch still loved Daemon Targaryen
note: Watch's loyalty to Daemon -> King's Landing falls

snippet: Willow Pound-Stone tore the weeping prince from his arms
note: Thorne's death then mob seizes Maelor then disputed killing

snippet: And thus did betrayal beget more betrayal
note: Mysaria's accusation to Addam's escape and Corlys imprisoned

snippet: never to be seen again at court or castle
note: Norren's warning to Nettles fleeing Maidenpool

snippet: the treasury was empty and the Crown's debt was mounting
note: empty treasury -> three foreign loans -> Dragonpit work resumed

snippet: rose up in red rebellion
note: Dalton's death -> fleet dissolves -> Fair Isle rebellion

snippet: the new Hand decreed that it should be a day
note: Unwin's decree → mass mutilations and executions before the Red Keep

snippet: The trap was neatly set
note: Alyn's Stepstones victory → Unwin sending him against the ironmen as a trap

snippet: the only truly plausible culprit: Unwin Peake
note: Jaehaera removed then king freed to remarry then Peake's succession scheme

snippet: On Maiden's Day we shall have a ball
note: Peake's rivals force the ball then the ball produces Daenaera not Myrielle

snippet: were the Hand and his minions responsible for these tragedies
note: rival maidens' misfortunes then suspicion of Peake's "Maiden's Day curse"

snippet: we have brought your new queen
note: the twins present Daenaera then king's choice undoes Peake's scheme

snippet: underestimated the will and determination of Alysanne Targaryen
note: betrothal to Orryn → Alysanne and Jaehaerys elope to Dragonstone
